ops.title: Pin the TCC identity blocking cookiesync cross-host pulls
ops.description: When a union pull from another Mac times out against this peer ("skipped <host>: no reply") and the peer's daemon is healthy, the likely cause is macOS TCC blocking the ssh-spawned cookiesync child from reading browser data. The gated identity depends on which transport dialed in: LAN sshd (Remote Login) has a stable identity; Tailscale SSH runs remote commands as children of tailscaled. Never assume which one — pin it from the TCC log, then grant Full Disk Access to the process the log names.

ops.text: On the PEER (the host being pulled from), start streaming the TCC subsystem BEFORE reproducing: log stream --predicate 'subsystem == "com.apple.TCC"'

From the other Mac, reproduce the failing pull: cookiesync cookies --all (or the failing command). Note the wall-clock second it starts; the union gives the peer 15s, so the interesting TCC lines land inside that window.
In the stream, find the deny/prompt line inside the 15s window and note the requesting process it names (sshd-child, tailscaled, cookiesync, or another binary).

Grant Full Disk Access to exactly that identity on the peer: System Settings > Privacy & Security > Full Disk Access. If the log named tailscaled, prefer also recording a LAN addr (synckitd host addr add) so pulls dial sshd first and the grant is stable across Tailscale updates.
Re-run the pull and confirm the peer's entries converge and the warning is gone; cookiesync doctor on the puller should show the peer TCC line only as an informational pointer.
